column216:
What to do in user mode

Use diverse sources and diff them

Call the usual module APIs (Toolhelp/PSAPI) and read the PEB’s loader lists directly. If they disagree, something’s meddling.

Walk virtual memory and list regions with image mappings (MEM_IMAGE) and PE headers; compare to the API/PEB lists. Memory-only (“manual map”) images will show up here even if hidden elsewhere.

Subscribe to ETW ImageLoad events to build a running ledger of what loaded; compare that timeline to your current snapshot.

Validate what you find

Code-signing checks: verify signatures on every image you see; flag unsigned/unexpected signers.

On-disk vs in-memory diffs: hash on-disk modules and compare to in-memory sections to catch inline patches.

Import/export sanity: a module that “exists” but has broken imports/sections is suspicious.

Detect tampering with your enumerators

Keep a clean, read-only image of ntdll (or known-good exports) and compare first bytes of critical functions you rely on; if they differ, you’re likely running under inline hooks.

Cross-call alternate code paths (e.g., different families of APIs that should report the same thing). Divergence is a signal.

Harden your own process (prevention helps detection)

Enable mitigation policies (e.g., blocking dynamic code, restricting image loading from writable locations) to reduce easy injections you’d otherwise have to find.

Maintain a per-build allowlist of expected modules and fail closed or degrade features when anything outside the baseline appears.

```
EBRW Guidance (Reading & Writing) - Main idea/structure: Identify purpose, tone, and how each sentence/paragraph functions. 
- Vocab-in-context: Replace the blank; pick the option that fits BOTH logic and tone. 
- Evidence-based: Prefer answers directly supported by the passage; avoid extreme or unstated claims. 
- Grammar & Usage (prioritize concision/clarity): • Subject–verb agreement (incl. inverted/interrupting clauses). • Pronoun–antecedent clarity (singular/plural, who/whom, it/they). • Modifier placement (avoid dangling/misplaced). • Parallelism (lists, comparisons, paired constructions). • Verb tense/sequence (timeline consistency). • Punctuation: comma for nonessential, semicolon joins 2 independent clauses, colon introduces explanation/list, dash for emphasis. • Idioms & prepositions (standard usage). - Transitions: Choose contrast/cause/example/sequence as demanded by context. 

Math Guidance - Translate words → equations; define variables clearly. - Units/constraints: nonnegativity, integer conditions, domain. - Algebra: isolate, factor, use equivalent forms; check extraneous roots. - Functions: slope/intercept, vertex, transformations, domain/range. - Systems: substitution/elimination; for optimization, test critical cases. - Exponents/logs: rules & conversions. - Geometry: write relationships; use similarity/parallel lines; angle/arc rules. - Data: read tables/graphs carefully; compute exactly what’s asked (mean/median, % change). - Always sanity-check magnitude and units.
```
